agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Lease of Coal Harbour Seawalk portion near 333 Menchions Mews
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider leasing an 11.8 sq m portion of the Coal Harbour Seawalk to 562270 B.C. Ltd. (owner of 333 Menchions Mews) for an enclosed lobby for Lift Bar & Grill. The proposed lease is for 10 years plus one 10-year renewal option, at $1,500 annual rent inclusive of property taxes. The lease would be retroactive to December 1, 2024, and includes a 12-month cancellation clause for municipal use.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on May 19, 2026 — this could affect public access to the seawalk.

key_dates.label: Council vote on Coal Harbour Seawalk lease

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Sign bylaw amendment for PNE Exhibition Park at 2901 East Hastings Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on May 19, 2026 on a proposed amendment to the Sign By-law to create a new Sign District for PNE Exhibition Park at 2901 East Hastings Street. If approved, the amendment would permit up to three new electronic static image signs with first-party and third-party advertising, and would also amend the Sign Fee By-law. The new district applies only to the PNE property and does not include city street rights-of-way.

key_dates.label: Council vote on PNE sign bylaw amendment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Capital grant for social housing at 1395 Rolston Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a capital grant of up to $30 million to More Than a Roof Mennonite Housing Society for construction of approximately 212 social housing units (52% family units) with retail at grade on City-owned land at 1395 Rolston Street. Approval requires at least 8 affirmative votes (two-thirds of Council) under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter. The grant will be disbursed once all permits and funding are in place, prior to construction start.

key_dates.label: Council vote on $30M social housing grant at 1395 Rolston Street

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver council to vote on large electronic advertising signs on streets
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the Street and Traffic By-law that would allow large-format electronic advertising signs on city streets. If approved, this would permit digital billboards and similar electronic signs in certain locations. The proposal is still under consideration and has not been approved yet. Business owners who use or are affected by street signage should be aware of this potential change.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your signage options or advertising costs.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on modifying conditions for West Georgia, Burrard, Alberni development
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on May 19, 2026 on a staff recommendation to extend the deadline for enacting the CD-1 By-law for four rezoning applications at 1450 West Georgia Street, 1157 Burrard Street, 1640-1650 Alberni Street, and 1668-1684 Alberni Street to May 31, 2028. The extension relates to conditions for a Community Amenity Contribution (CAC). If approved, the change would give developers an additional two years to finalize the bylaw enactment, potentially delaying construction timelines and related impacts on nearby businesses.

agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote May 19, 2026 on bylaw amendments to add a new RR-3C residential rental district, repeal the RR-2C district, and rezone four properties: 4708-4742 Joyce St & 3604 Tanner St to RR-2B; 4471-4485 Fraser St & 691 E 29th Ave to RR-3C; 2005-2045 W 49th Ave to RR-2B; and 1401-1455 E 49th Ave to RR-3C. The changes also update parking, sign, noise, and zoning bylaws to include RR-3C and remove RR-2C. In RR-2B, maximum floor space ratio increases to 3.00 if 20% of floor area is below-market rental or 100% is social housing, and building height increases to 6 storeys (8 in transit-oriented areas). The 20% below-market requirement for RR-2C is removed.

agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has enacted By-law amendments creating a new C-2A mixed-use commercial district that fast-tracks approvals for rental housing and small hotels. minutes_briefs.title: Council approved three new electronic signs at PNE Exhibition Park
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council unanimously approved an amendment to the Sign By-law to allow the Pacific National Exhibition (PNE) to install three new electronic static image signs at 2901 East Hastings Street (PNE Exhibition Park). minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $30M grant for 212 social housing units on Rolston Street
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council unanimously approved a $30 million capital grant to More Than a Roof Mennonite Housing Society (MTAR) to build about 212 social housing units on city-owned land at 1395 Rolston Street. The grant comes from the Vancouver Affordable Housing Endowment Fund (VAHEF) Capital Budget, with $30 million transferred from the Community Amenity Contributions reserve. Council also authorized the City to enter into a Grant Agreement with MTAR on terms set out in the February 4, 2026 report. The decision does not limit Council or City officials in making future decisions about zoning, subdivision, or permits for the property. No legal rights or obligations will arise until all legal documents are signed.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 6-storey rental building at 717-743 West 28th
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council voted to approve a rezoning application for 717-743 West 28th Avenue. The property will be rezoned from RM-8A (Residential) to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development) District. This allows the developer to build a six-storey residential rental building with 94 units. The new rules increase the maximum floor space ratio (FSR) — the amount of floor area allowed compared to the lot size — from 1.2 to 3.0. The maximum building height also goes up from 11.5 metres (about 38 feet) to 21 metres (69 feet). The approval is in principle, meaning the developer must still meet several conditions before the final bylaw is passed. These conditions include signing a Housing Agreement to keep the units as rental housing for a set period. Council also instructed the Director of Legal Services to prepare that Housing Agreement bylaw.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own or develop property nearby, review the new CD-1 zoning rules to understand how they may affect your future development plans.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for two 22-storey rental buildings on West 6th
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council approved a rezoning application for 2244-2280 West 6th Avenue. The property will be rezoned from R5-4 (Residential) to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development) District. This allows the developer to build two 22-storey residential buildings with 416 rental units. At least 20% of the residential floor area will be set aside as below-market rental units. The maximum floor space ratio (FSR) — the ratio of a building's total floor area to the size of the land it sits on — will be 6.5. The approval is subject to conditions, including a Housing Agreement that locks in the below-market units.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 17-storey rental tower on Main Street
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council approved a rezoning application for 1880-1898 Main Street and 1851 Lorne Street. The property was rezoned from IC-1 (Industrial) to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development) District. This allows a 17-storey mixed-use building with 185 rental homes, including 20% below-market rental units, and ground-floor commercial space. The maximum floor space ratio (FSR) — the ratio of total floor area to lot size — was increased from 3.0 to 8.0, and the maximum building height was increased from 18.3 metres (60 feet) to 51.2 metres (168 feet). Council also approved the draft CD-1 bylaw, the form of development, and instructed staff to prepare a Housing Agreement Bylaw to secure the below-market rental units. The Sign Bylaw and Noise Control Bylaw will be amended to match the new zoning.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved new RR-3C district to fast-track rental housing
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council voted to create a new residential rental district called RR-3C, which is designed to speed up approvals for rental housing. The old RR-2C district was eliminated. Council also approved rezoning several specific properties to the new RR-3C or to the existing RR-2B district, including sites on Joyce Street, Fraser Street, and West 49th Avenue. These changes update the city's zoning and development bylaw, subdivision bylaw, sign bylaw, noise control bylaw, and parking bylaw to support faster rental housing construction.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own or are developing one of the rezoned properties, contact the City's Planning department to understand how the new RR-3C rules affect your project.
